summary: 11 countries. The US war with Iran leads coverage almost everywhere as US Central Command wraps an eleventh night of strikes and Trump threatens to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for every attack on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Tariffs run a close second, with fresh US duties on Brazil taking effect and a 30 percent rate hanging over Mexico and the EU.

12 countries. The eleventh straight night of US strikes on Iran leads coverage nearly everywhere, with Washington now putting the war's cost at $37.5 billion and Trump saying he has no interest in talks for now. Tariff fights are the second thread, from Canada and Mexico to Brazil and the African trade programme AGOA.

12 countries. The reignited US-Iran war dominates coverage abroad: US forces pressed a tenth day of strikes as the American death toll reached 17, Iran called it a full-scale war, and Trump vowed Tehran would pay. New 50% US tariffs on Canadian goods and Trump's revived election-fraud claims against China also travel widely.

11 countries. The deepening US-Iran war leads coverage abroad: the US completed a ninth straight night of strikes as the American death toll reached 17 and Iran's leadership called the ceasefire worthless. The World Cup final outside New York, where Trump was booed while handing Spain the trophy over Argentina, and his revived China election-fraud claims also travel widely.

11 countries. Trump's Thursday night primetime address dominates coverage: he accused China of the largest compromise of election data in history, said Beijing took files on 220 million US voters and ordered intelligence declassified, all while reviving his 2020 stolen-election claims. Beijing calls the charges fabricated. New visa limits on foreign students and journalists, and the continuing US-Iran fight over the Strait of Hormuz, also travel abroad.

11 countries. The renewed US-Iran war over the Strait of Hormuz dominates coverage, with fresh US strikes, a reimposed naval blockade and Iranian attacks on Gulf states. Trump's reversal on halting ICE traffic stops and a White House move to release intelligence on China also travel abroad.
11 countries. The US-Iran clash over the Strait of Hormuz, a renewed naval blockade and fresh strikes dominate world coverage. A US Supreme Court ruling against Trump's tariffs also lands abroad.
9 countries · Iran strikes & Hormuz blockade dominate world coverage · US 250th anniversary
